title: Bolbitis hydrophylla
intro: Bolbitis hydrophylla is a type of aquatic fern commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds and ability to thrive in submerged aquatic environments. The health benefits of Bolbitis hydrophylla include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bolbitis hydrophylla is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and capsules, often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal conditions, as well as to support immune system function.
taxonomy: Bolbitis hydrophylla is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This genus of ferns is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Bolbitis hydrophylla is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which comprises approximately 3,000 species of ferns. The family Polypodiaceae is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Bolbitis hydrophylla's classification reflects its evolutionary position within the fern lineage.

It is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses.
